opp.name: RiotGames_einvoicing_June'26
opp.next_step: 6.22.26 KK - no response or movement after initial call. Shared followup asking about the RFPs and next steps. Invited to horizon Zycus to send the corporate deck and relevant product information. Mozammil/team to share the eInvoicing RFP or report with Zycus. Set up a 30-minute presales/demo session focused on eInvoicing, eSourcing, and autonomous agent sourcing. Validate Oracle, Coupa, Slack, and Google integration feasibility. Position Zycus as a modular entry point now and broader S2P/AI partner later.
opp.ais_why:
• Riot Games is comparing 'disparate procurement systems' and wants 'clear functional differentiation between intake/orchestration vendors,' with interest in eInvoicing as a standalone module.
• Agentic AI and Merlin are scoped, so AI-enabled orchestration is in scope, but the buyer's stated focus is orchestration fit and module flexibility.
• No agentic discussion or AI sentiment is captured, keeping appetite mid-range and evaluation-driven.

items.quote: Mozammil asked on 11 June call whether Zycus is a Teams shop or Slack/Google shop, noting the intake demo shown was Microsoft Teams-based. Riot uses Slack and Google Workspace.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11; prior sweep risk memory

items.area: AI agent architecture uncertainty: Zycus platform-native vs generic AI platform (Gemini Vertex, Workato)
items.quote: Mozammil asked technical questions on 11 June about whether AI agents should live inside a procurement platform (Zycus) or a generic AI platform like Gemini Vertex or Workato, signaling buyer uncertainty about Zycus's technical approach and integration effort required.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11; prior sweep risk memory

items.area: eInvoicing RFP competitive landscape and formal decision criteria unknown
items.quote: Mozammil agreed on 11 June to send the eInvoicing RFP but has not delivered it as of 2026-06-22. Without the RFP, Zycus does not know the formal evaluation criteria, scoring weights, competitor list, or submission timeline.

metrics.narrative: Mozammil articulated qualitative pain on the 11 June discovery call around manual invoice processing complexity and the need for intake orchestration across multiple procurement touchpoints (Slack, Google, email). He stated Riot is evaluating eight vendors for intake/orchestration and separately has an eInvoicing RFP in flight. However, no quantified business case was provided: no cost of manual processing, headcount burden, cycle time impact, error rate, or savings target. The lack of quantified metrics at this stage is a yellow flag—without a business case, commercial conversations and EB approval remain blocked. Zycus should use the promised 30-minute presales/demo session to extract quantified pain and ROI targets, anchoring both the eInvoicing and intake/orchestration value propositions to measurable outcomes.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole (zero roles, no champion identified)
champion.narrative: Mozammil Aslam is the only identified contact, but he does not yet qualify as a strong or even developing champion. He engaged in a single discovery call (11 June), asked detailed technical questions (Slack/Google integration, AI agent architecture, Oracle/Coupa feasibility), and agreed to three commitments: (1) send the eInvoicing RFP, (2) schedule a 30-minute presales/demo session, and (3) review the corporate deck and intake/orchestration screenshots Zycus would provide. However, as of 22 June (9 days ago), he has not responded to Karson's follow-up, has not delivered the RFP, and has not scheduled the demo. This signals passive engagement or deprioritization. There is no evidence of advocacy: Mozammil has not introduced Zycus to the EB, DM, or other stakeholders; has not shared internal documents or RFP scoring criteria; and has not pushed back on competitors. His role appears to be functional evaluator, not business sponsor, so even if he becomes an advocate, he lacks the authority to drive the deal. Champion strength is weak and cooling. Risk: if Mozammil fully disengages, Zycus has zero alternative threads into Riot. Move: escalate to Mozammil's manager or a senior procurement/finance leader to bypass the single-threaded dependency and identify a true business champion with EB access.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11, Mozammil statement on 8-vendor evaluation
Known competitors from prior sweep: Globality, GEP, Coupa, Ariba, 8 unnamed intake/orchestration vendors
competition.narrative: Riot is running a highly competitive, multi-vendor evaluation. Mozammil stated on the 11 June call that Riot is 'very late in an evaluation of eight vendors' for intake/orchestration, and separately has an eInvoicing RFP in flight. Known named competitors from prior sweep memory and Salesforce synthesis include Globality, GEP, Coupa, and Ariba, plus eight unnamed intake/orchestration vendors. The intake/orchestration competitive set is advanced—Riot has already narrowed to a shortlist, and Zycus appears to have entered late or been excluded from that primary cycle. The eInvoicing RFP is Zycus's proposed entry point, but the competitive landscape for that RFP is unknown (Mozammil has not shared the RFP document or named the eInvoicing competitors). Sentiment is neutral to negative: Mozammil's 9-day silence after the discovery call suggests Zycus is not top-of-mind, and the late entry into the intake/orchestration evaluation positions Zycus as an underdog. How Zycus wins: (1) use the eInvoicing RFP as a modular entry point to demonstrate quick value, then expand to broader S2P and AI orchestration; (2) position Merlin agentic AI as the orchestration layer that sits above the 8-vendor intake evaluation, making Zycus a strategic AI partner rather than a point-solution competitor; (3) prove Slack/Google integration depth with a reference story or proof-of-concept to overcome the Teams-based demo concern; (4) secure EB alignment to bypass the functional evaluation layer where Zycus is late-stage. The wedge is AI-first orchestration and modular deployment; the activation is delivering the eInvoicing RFP response and demo immediately to prove speed-to-value.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11, Mozammil statements on manual invoice processing and intake orchestration pain
identify_pain.narrative: Mozammil articulated clear operational pain on the 11 June discovery call, centered on two areas: (1) manual invoice processing complexity, which is driving the eInvoicing RFP, and (2) intake orchestration challenges across multiple procurement touchpoints—Slack, Google Workspace, email, and likely other channels—leading to fragmented requisition and approval workflows. He stated Riot is evaluating eight vendors for intake/orchestration to solve the latter pain point, indicating this is a high-priority operational issue. The pain owner appears to be Mozammil's team (procurement operations or technology), though the ultimate business owner (the EB or sponsor funding this initiative) has not been identified. The pain is qualitative at this stage—no quantified cost of manual processing, cycle time, or error rate was provided—but the urgency is implied by the advanced 8-vendor evaluation and the parallel eInvoicing RFP. Zycus is positioned to address both pain points (eInvoicing via the core platform, intake orchestration via Merlin agentic AI), but without a quantified business case or EB alignment, the pain remains unmonetized. Move: use the presales/demo to extract specific pain metrics (invoices per month, manual touch time, approval cycle delays, error rate) and tie them to ROI.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11 (no paper process discussion)
paper_process.narrative: No contracting or procurement mechanics have been discussed. The 11 June discovery call focused entirely on technical fit (integrations, AI architecture, product scope), and no MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec requirements, or procurement channel details were surfaced. Given Riot's size (major gaming company) and the scope of this initiative (multi-product S2P transformation with AI agents), a formal procurement process with InfoSec review, legal redlining, and likely a Master Services Agreement is certain, but none of this has been scoped. No SI or implementation partner was mentioned, and no contract expiration forcing function exists (Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty). The paper process is a complete unknown at this Qualified stage, which is normal for early discovery but becomes a blocker if Zycus advances without understanding Riot's procurement requirements. Move: during the presales/demo session, ask Mozammil to outline Riot's procurement and legal review process, InfoSec requirements, and whether an SI or implementation partner will be involved. Use the eInvoicing RFP delivery as the trigger to surface these mechanics.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. Mozammil Aslam is a functional evaluator (likely procurement operations or technology role based on the technical depth of his questions about AI agents, integrations, and orchestration platforms), but he has not indicated budget authority or commercial decision-making power. Zero contact roles are logged in Salesforce, and no EB or Executive Sponsor was present on the 11 June discovery call. Without EB alignment, Zycus cannot advance pricing, negotiate commercial terms, or secure final approval regardless of technical fit. The evidence shows Riot is running a multi-vendor evaluation with an eInvoicing RFP, both of which would require C-level or VP-level budget sign-off given the scope (intake orchestration across 8 vendors, plus eInvoicing transformation). Immediate move: Karson or the deal owner's manager should request an introduction to the procurement or finance leader who controls the budget for this initiative, using the eInvoicing RFP delivery as the hook.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11, Mozammil statement on 8-vendor evaluation
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is fragmented across two parallel evaluations. Mozammil stated on 11 June that Riot is 'very late in an evaluation of eight vendors' for intake/orchestration, meaning a shortlist has likely been formed and Zycus was not included in the primary cycle. Separately, Riot has an eInvoicing RFP in flight, which Mozammil agreed to share but has not delivered. The call sequence shows only one discovery call (11 June) with Mozammil as the sole participant—no EB, DM, or executive sponsor has joined, and no follow-up demo or commercial discussion has been scheduled despite Mozammil's agreement to a 30-minute presales session. This suggests Riot's process is buyer-led and vendor-gated: Zycus is waiting for Mozammil to grant access (via the RFP and demo invite), but he has not responded to the 22 June follow-up. The approval chain is unknown—Salesforce fields for purchase approvals, multiple approvals, and EB identification are all empty. Riot's process appears to be: (1) intake/orchestration evaluation (advanced, 8 vendors, Zycus late or excluded); (2) eInvoicing RFP (stage unknown, Zycus invited but not yet engaged); (3) integration and AI architecture validation (Mozammil's technical diligence). Without visibility into who approves the budget and timeline, Zycus is flying blind. Move: escalate to Mozammil's manager or the procurement/finance leader to understand the real decision timeline and approval path.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11, Mozammil questions on integrations and AI architecture
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-22 (eInvoicing RFP not yet delivered)
decision_criteria.narrative: Mozammil outlined several technical and functional evaluation criteria on the 11 June call, signaling what Riot is prioritizing across the intake/orchestration and eInvoicing evaluations. Confirmed criteria include: (1) Slack and Google Workspace integration (Riot's collaboration stack), contrasted against the Teams-based demo Zycus showed; (2) Oracle ERP and Coupa integration feasibility, which Mozammil explicitly asked to validate; (3) AI agent architecture and whether agents should live inside the procurement platform (Zycus) or a generic AI platform like Gemini Vertex or Workato; (4) modular/phased deployment, as Mozammil expressed interest in starting with eInvoicing and expanding to broader S2P and AI later. However, the formal decision criteria for the eInvoicing RFP have not been shared—Mozammil agreed to send the RFP document but has not delivered it as of 2026-06-22. The criteria for the 8-vendor intake/orchestration evaluation remain unknown. Zycus is partially aligned on modularity and AI positioning but exposed on Slack/Google integration depth. Next move: secure the eInvoicing RFP to see the formal criteria and prepare a Slack/Google integration proof-of-concept or reference story.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-11, currently 20 days in Qualified stage. Close date 2027-06-11 is 345 days out, so typical early-cycle long runway. However time-in-stage math shows no forward movement: one discovery call 2026-06-11, rep follow-up 2026-06-22, then 9 days of buyer silence. No submission dates recorded for Formal Eval, Shortlisted, or other gates. Forward slip risk is moderate: buyer stated on 11 June they are 'very late in an evaluation of eight vendors' for intake/orchestration, meaning Riot's primary decision cycle is far ahead of this opp's Qualified stage. Pace required: immediate EB mapping and eInvoicing RFP delivery to validate whether Zycus has a real entry point, or this remains a future-cycle placeholder.
evidence: Engagement state: LIVE per server pulse, LastActivityDate 2026-06-22 (9 days ago), but buyer has not responded to rep outreach or delivered the eInvoicing RFP promised on the 11 June call.

No quantified business case or EB alignment: pain articulated (manual invoice processing, intake orchestration complexity) but no quantified impact, no budget authority mapped.

Risk: Zycus missed the primary selection window and is attempting entry via eInvoicing RFP, but buyer has not delivered the RFP or scheduled the promised demo.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning partially strong on product scope (Merlin agentic AI, autonomous agent sourcing included in Products__c and Next_Step__c), but buyer questioning AI agent architecture (Zycus platform-native vs Gemini Vertex/Workato). Mozammil engaged with AI narrative on 11 June call, but no follow-through to validate or demo. Under-positioned risk: Zycus has not yet proven the AI orchestration wedge that differentiates from the 8-vendor intake/orchestration shortlist.

items.quote: Mozammil asked on 11 June call whether Zycus is a Teams shop or Slack/Google shop, noting the intake demo shown was Microsoft Teams-based.
items.criterion: Slack and Google Workspace integration (Riot's collaboration stack)

items.quote: Mozammil explicitly asked on 11 June to validate Oracle and Coupa integration feasibility; Next_Step__c includes this as a validation item.
items.criterion: Oracle ERP and Coupa integration feasibility

items.quote: Mozammil asked technical questions on 11 June about whether AI agents should live inside a procurement platform (Zycus) or a generic AI platform like Gemini Vertex or Workato, signaling buyer uncertainty about Zycus's technical approach.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11; prior sweep risk memory
items.criterion: AI agent architecture: platform-native (Zycus) vs generic AI platform (Gemini Vertex, Workato)

items.quote: Next_Step__c 2026-06-22 states: 'Position Zycus as a modular entry point now and broader S2P/AI partner later.' Mozammil's agreement to review eInvoicing RFP separately from intake/orchestration evaluation signals buyer openness to phased approach.
items.criterion: Modular/phased deployment: eInvoicing entry point, expand to S2P + AI later

items.quote: Mozammil stated on 11 June: 'We are very late in an evaluation of eight vendors' for intake/orchestration, indicating Zycus missed the primary selection window and is attempting entry via eInvoicing RFP.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11; prior sweep risk memory
items.criterion: Intake/orchestration vendor selection timeline: Riot 'very late' in 8-vendor evaluation

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Partially aligned on product scope (eInvoicing, iSource, Merlin agentic AI) and modular deployment philosophy, but exposed on Slack/Google integration depth (demo was Teams-based) and AI agent architecture (buyer questioning whether agents should live in Zycus vs Gemini Vertex/Workato). Late entry into 8-vendor intake/orchestration evaluation is a major misalignment—Riot's primary decision cycle is ahead of Zycus's engagement timeline.

hard.ais_why:
• Riot Games is comparing 'disparate procurement systems' and wants 'clear functional differentiation between intake/orchestration vendors,' with interest in eInvoicing as a standalone module.
• Agentic AI and Merlin are scoped, so AI-enabled orchestration is in scope, but the buyer's stated focus is orchestration fit and module flexibility.
• No agentic discussion or AI sentiment is captured, keeping appetite mid-range and evaluation-driven.
hard.next_step:
6.22.26 KK - no response or movement after initial call. Shared followup asking about the RFPs and next steps. Invited to horizon


Zycus to send the corporate deck and relevant product information.

Mozammil/team to share the eInvoicing RFP or report with Zycus.

Set up a 30-minute presales/demo session focused on eInvoicing, eSourcing, and autonomous agent sourcing.

Validate Oracle, Coupa, Slack, and Google integration feasibility.

Position Zycus as a modular entry point now and broader S2P/AI partner later.

value.name: Globality
value.quote: Mozammil stated on 11 June that Riot is 'very late in an evaluation of eight vendors' for intake/orchestration; Globality known from prior sweep as one of the named competitors.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11; prior sweep competitor memory
value.how_we_win: Position Merlin agentic AI as orchestration layer that integrates above Globality's intake point solution; prove AI-first negotiation and sourcing agents deliver ROI beyond intake automation.

packets.subject: Globality

value.name: GEP
value.quote: GEP known from prior sweep as named competitor in the intake/orchestration evaluation.
value.source: Prior sweep competitor memory
value.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's full S2P + AI platform vs GEP's consulting-heavy model; emphasize modular eInvoicing entry point with faster time-to-value and lower implementation risk.

value.name: GEP
value.quote: GEP — previously used for sourcing/CLM at Riot and later sunsetted; cited as practitioner-focused. Dissatisfaction with GEP CLM: perceived as built for procurement practitioners, not end users; lacked guided UX and innovation, prompting migration.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 11 June, Mozammil Aslam (speaker_id 2)
value.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus guided UX and business-user focus vs GEP's practitioner-only design; emphasize continuous innovation (Merlin AI, agentic features) vs GEP's stagnation.

packets.subject: GEP

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Mozammil asked on 11 June to validate Coupa integration feasibility, indicating Riot uses Coupa and integration is a decision criterion. Coupa also known from prior sweep as named competitor.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-11; prior sweep competitor memory
value.how_we_win: Prove Zycus integrates seamlessly with Coupa (Oracle, ERP, Slack, Google) as a complementary layer; position as AI orchestration partner that enhances Coupa rather than replaces it, reducing change management risk.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Coupa — used for P2P module at Riot (downstream), Riot moved off Coupa CLM due to lack of innovation.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 11 June, Mozammil Aslam (speaker_id 2)
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the innovation-led alternative; highlight Merlin AI and autonomous agent vs Coupa's lack of AI/innovation. Coupa P2P is entrenched, so focus on eSourcing and intake/orchestration displacement.

packets.subject: Coupa

value.quote: Ariba known from prior sweep as named competitor.
value.source: Prior sweep competitor memory
value.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's AI-first agentic approach (Merlin negotiation, autonomous sourcing) vs Ariba's legacy workflow automation; emphasize Slack/Google native integration vs SAP's Microsoft-centric stack.

value.detail: AI agent architecture uncertainty: Mozammil asked technical questions on 11 June about whether AI agents should live inside a procurement platform (Zycus) or a generic AI platform like Gemini Vertex or Workato. This signals buyer uncertainty about Zycus's technical approach and integration effort required.

Intake/orchestration 8-vendor evaluation: vendor names (beyond Globality, GEP, Coupa, Ariba from prior memory), shortlist status, and decision timeline unknown (knowledge gap).
No quantified business case: no cost of manual invoice processing, headcount burden, cycle time, error rate, or ROI target provided (knowledge gap).
Approval chain and paper process unknown: no visibility into who approves budget, MSA/SOW requirements, InfoSec review, or procurement channel (knowledge gap).
